name: cv-net-library

metadata.name: cv-net-library
metadata.summary: A library of complex-valued neural networks

documentation:
# cv_net_library

---

The idea of this library is  to implement Complex class, 
everything else stays the same as any PyTorch code.

I am basically working with Complex-Valued Neural Networks.  In the need of making our coding more dynamic we build this
library not to have to repeat the same code over and over and accelerate the speed of coding.

## Installation

### Using [PIP]

Only use complex-valued neural networks library:

`pip install cv-net-library`

### Using GitHub

Useful if you want to modify the source code and view the relevant tests:

`https://github.com/maple18661959396/ISAR_CV_NET`

---

## To Use

Import the cv_net_library base class. then Import related classes or functions from the corresponding module.

The functions in cv_net_library has the same function as the corresponding function in Pytorch, 
and the prefix Complex is added before the corresponding function. as the following modules.

If you have any inquiries regarding the functionality or parameters of any function,
please visit GitHub to examine the original source code.

### layer

- ComplexLayers

  `ComplexConv2d` `ComplexConv1d` `ComplexConv3d` `ComplexFlatten`  `ComplexConvTransposed2d`  `ComplexLinear`

- ComplexDropout

  `ComplexDropout2D` `ComplexDropout` `ComplexDropoutRespectively`

- ComplexPooling

  `ComplexAvgPool1D` `ComplexAvgPool2D` `ComplexAvgPool3D` `ComplexPolarAvgPooling2D` `ComplexMaxPool2D`  `ComplexUnPooling2D`

- ComplexUpSampooling

  `ComplexUpSampling` `ComplexUpSamplingBilinear2d` `ComplexUpSamplingNearest2d`
  

### function

- ComplexBatchNorm

  `ComplexBatchNorm` `ComplexBatchNorm1d` `ComplexBatchNorm2d`

### activation

- ComplexActivations

  `complex_relu` `complex_elu` `complex_exponential` `complex_sigmoid` `complex_tanh` `complex_hard_sigmoid`
  `complex_leaky_relu` `complex_selu` `complex_softplus` `complex_softsign` `complex_softmax`
  `modrelu` `zrelu` `complex_cardioid` `sigmoid_real` `softmax_real_with_abs` `softmax_real_with_avg`
  `softmax_real_with_mult` `softmax_of_softmax_real_with_mult` `softmax_of_softmax_real_with_avg`
  `softmax_real_by_parameter` `softmax_real_with_polar` `georgiou_cdbp` `complex_signum`
  `mvn_activation` `apply_pol` `pol_tanh` `pol_sigmoid` `pol_selu`

### loss

- ComplexLoss

  `ComplexAverageCrossEntropy`  `ComplexAverageCrossEntropyAbs` `ComplexMeanSquareError` 
  `ComplexAverageCrossEntropyIgnoreUnlabeled`  `ComplexWeightedAverageCrossEntropy`
  `ComplexWeightedAverageCrossEntropyIgnoreUnlabeled` `FrobeniusLoss` `ComplexL1Loss` `ComplexSmoothL1Loss`
  `ComplexNLLLoss` `ComplexNLLLossAbs`

## Example

```
# Make a A-ConvNets 
import torch.nn as nn
import cv_net_library
from cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation import complex_relu, complex_softmax
from cv_net_library.layer.ComplexLayers import ComplexLinear, ComplexConv2d
from cv_net_library.layer.ComplexDropout import ComplexDropout2D
from cv_net_library.layer.ComplexPooling import ComplexMaxPool2D
from cv_net_library.layer.ComplexUpSampling import ComplexUpSamplingBilinear2d
from cv_net_library.layer.ComplexLayers import ComplexLinear, ComplexConv2d
from cv_net_library.function.ComplexBatchNorm import ComplexBatchNorm2d, ComplexBatchNorm1d
from cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss import ComplexAverageCrossEntropy, ComplexAverageCrossEntropyAbs

class ComplexNet(nn.Module):

    def __init__(self):
        super(ComplexNet, self).__init__()
        self.conv1 = ComplexConv2d(1, 16, 13, 1)
        self.bn2d1 = ComplexBatchNorm2d(16, track_running_stats=False)
        self.maxpool1 = ComplexMaxPool2D(2, 2)
        self.conv2 = ComplexConv2d(16, 32, 13, 1)
        self.bn2d2 = ComplexBatchNorm2d(32, track_running_stats=False)
        self.maxpool2 = ComplexMaxPool2D(2, 2)
        self.conv3 = ComplexConv2d(32, 64, 12, 1)
        self.bn2d3 = ComplexBatchNorm2d(64, track_running_stats=False)
        self.maxpool3 = ComplexMaxPool2D(2, 2)
        self.dropout1 = ComplexDropout2D(p=0.5)
        self.conv4 = ComplexConv2d(64, 128, 10, 1)
        self.bn2d4 = ComplexBatchNorm2d(128, track_running_stats=False)
        self.conv5 = ComplexConv2d(128, 7, 6, 1)
        self.bn2d5 = ComplexBatchNorm2d(7, track_running_stats=False)

    def forward(self, x):
        x = self.conv1(x)
        x = self.bn2d1(x)
        x = complex_relu(x)
        x = self.maxpool1(x)
        x = self.conv2(x)
        x = self.bn2d2(x)
        x = complex_relu(x)
        x = self.maxpool2(x)
        x = self.conv3(x)
        x = self.bn2d3(x)
        x = complex_relu(x)
        x = self.maxpool3(x)
        x = self.dropout1(x)
        x = self.conv4(x)
        x = self.bn2d4(x)
        x = complex_relu(x)
        x = self.conv5(x)
        x = self.bn2d5(x)
        x = x.view(x.shape[0], -1)
        x = complex_softmax(x, 1)
        return x
```

---
## Update Log
`0.0.5` Added some complex loss functions, fix bug

functions.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexLayers.apply_complex_conv
documentation:
fr(input.real)：The real part of the convolution kernel * The real part of the input。
fi(input.imag)：The  imaginary part of the convolution kernel * The  imaginary part of the input
fr(input.imag)：The real part of the convolution kernel * The  imaginary part of the input
fi(input.real)：The  imaginary part of the convolution kernel * The real part of the input

functions.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexDropout.complex_dropout_respectively
documentation: need to have the same dropout mask for real and imaginary part

functions.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexDropout.complex_dropout2d
documentation: dropout of real part and virtual part is regarded as adding noise interference

classes.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexDropout.ComplexDropout
documentation: Randomly zero out entire channels (a channel is a 2D feature map).

classes.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexUpSampling.ComplexUpSampling
documentation: Use the torch.nn.functional.interpolate function in the real and imaginary parts, respectively.

methods.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexUpSampling.ComplexUpSamplingBilinear2d.__init__
documentation: mode='bilinear' specifically for 2D

methods.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexUpSampling.ComplexUpSamplingNearest2d.__init__
documentation: mode='nearest' specifically for 2D

functions.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexPooling.complex_avg_pool2d
documentation: Use the torch.nn.functional.avg_pool2d function in the real and imaginary parts, respectively.

functions.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexPooling.complex_avg_pool3d
documentation: Use the torch.nn.functional.avg_pool2d function in the real and imaginary parts, respectively.

functions.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexPooling.complex_polar_avg_pooling2d
documentation: Use the torch.nn.functional.avg_pool3d function in the real and imaginary parts, respectively.

classes.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexPooling.ComplexAvgPool1D
documentation: AvgPooling under polar coordinates input

classes.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexPooling.ComplexMaxPool2D
documentation: Use the torch.nn.functional.max_pool2d function in the module of the complex number

classes.name: cv_net_library.layer.ComplexPooling.ComplexUnPooling2D
documentation: Use the torch.nn.functional.max_unpool2d function in the real and imaginary parts, respectively.

classes.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexAverageCrossEntropyAbs
documentation: ComplexAverageCrossEntropy Use the torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy in the real and imaginary parts, respectively.
Then take average

classes.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexMeanSquareError
documentation: Input complex tensor take modulo values then Use the torch.nn.functional.cross_entropy.

classes.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexAverageCrossEntropyIgnoreUnlabeled

methods.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexAverageCrossEntropyIgnoreUnlabeled.forward
documentation: Remove the unlabeled part of the corresponding sample
return F.mse_loss(torch.abs(y_true), torch.abs(y_pred), reduction=self.reduction)
The absolute value of the difference, not the difference of the absolute value.

methods.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexWeightedAverageCrossEntropy.__init__
documentation: The weight is updated by the given weight and the true value

methods.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexWeightedAverageCrossEntropyIgnoreUnlabeled.__init__
documentation: unlabeled part is removed then weighted according to the corresponding weight

classes.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexL1Loss
documentation: The Frobenius norm of the difference between the output and the target divided by the Frobenius norm of the target.

classes.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexSmoothL1Loss
documentation: F.l1_loss supports real-valued and complex-valued inputs.

classes.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexNLLLoss
documentation: F.smooth_l1_loss supports real-valued and complex-valued inputs.

classes.name: cv_net_library.loss.ComplexLoss.ComplexNLLLossAbs
documentation: ComplexNLLLoss Use the torch.nn.functional.nll_loss in the real and imaginary parts, respectively. Then take average

methods.name: cv_net_library.function.ComplexBatchNorm.ComplexBatchNorm.__init__
documentation: Method described in the paper
Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training by Reducing Internal Covariate Shift.
BatchNorm to normalize the data for each batch into consistent distribution
This class simply defines how the relevant parameters are set for different inputs

methods.name: cv_net_library.function.ComplexBatchNorm.ComplexBatchNorm1d.forward
documentation: Applies Batch Normalization over a 2D or 3D input.

methods.name: cv_net_library.function.ComplexBatchNorm.ComplexBatchNorm2d.forward
documentation: Applies Batch Normalization over a 4D input.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_elu
documentation: Apply the ReLU activation function to both the real and imaginary parts separately.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_exponential
documentation: Apply the elu activation function to both the real and imaginary parts separately.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_hard_sigmoid
documentation: F.tanh has been deprecated, use torch.tanh.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_selu

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_softplus
documentation: Apply the selu activation function to both the real and imaginary parts separately.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_softsign
documentation: Apply the softplus activation function to both the real and imaginary parts separately.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_softmax
documentation: Apply the softsign activation function to both the real and imaginary parts separately.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.modrelu
documentation:
mod ReLU presented in "Unitary Evolution Recurrent Neural Networks"
    from M. Arjovsky et al. (2016)
    URL: https://arxiv.org/abs/1511.06464
A variation of the ReLU named modReLU. It is a pointwise nonlinearity,
modReLU(inp) : C -> C, which affects only the absolute
value of a complex number, defined:
    modReLU(inp) = ReLU(|inp|+b)*inp/|inp|
TODO: See how to check the non zero abs.
Apply the softmax activation function to both the real and imaginary parts separately.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.zrelu
documentation:
zReLU presented in "On Complex Valued Convolutional Neural Networks"
    from Nitzan Guberman (2016).
This methods let's the output as the input if both real and imaginary parts are positive.

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/49412717/advanced-custom-activation-function-in-keras-tensorflow
modReLU(z) = ReLU(|z|+b)*z/|z|

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_cardioid
documentation:
Complex cardioid presented in "Better than Real: Complex-valued Neural Nets for MRI Fingerprinting"
    from V. Patrick (2017).

This function maintains the phase information while attenuating the magnitude based on the phase itself.
For real-valued inputs, it reduces to the ReLU.
Let the output be the input if both the real and imaginary parts are positive.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.sigmoid_real
documentation: This function maintains the phase information while attenuating the magnitude based on the phase itself.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.softmax_real_with_abs
documentation:
Applies the softmax function to the modulus of inp.
The softmax activation function transforms the outputs so that all values are in range (0, 1) and sum to 1.
It is often used as the activation for the last layer of a classification network because the result could be
interpreted as a probability distribution.
\:param inp: Input tensor.
\:return: Real-valued tensor of the applied activation function
Use the sigmoid activation function for the sum of the real and imaginary parts

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.softmax_real_with_avg
documentation:
The softmax activation function transforms the outputs so that all values are in range (0, 1) and sum to 1.
It is often used as the activation for the last layer of a classification network because the result could be
interpreted as a probability distribution.
\:param inp: Input tensor.
\:return: Real-valued tensor of the applied activation function
Use the sigmoid activation function for |inp|

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.softmax_real_with_mult
documentation:
The softmax activation function transforms the outputs so that all values are in range (0, 1) and sum to 1.
It is often used as the activation for the last layer of a classification network because the result could be
interpreted as a probability distribution.
\:param inp: Input tensor.
\:return: Real-valued tensor of the applied activation function
Use the softmax activation function in the real and imaginary parts, respectively.Then take the average

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.softmax_of_softmax_real_with_mult
documentation:
The softmax activation function transforms the outputs so that all values are in range (0, 1) and sum to 1.
It is often used as the activation for the last layer of a classification network because the result could be
interpreted as a probability distribution.
\:param inp: Input tensor.
\:return: Real-valued tensor of the applied activation function
Apply the softmax activation function to both the real and imaginary parts separately. Then, multiply the values

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.softmax_of_softmax_real_with_avg
documentation:
The softmax activation function transforms the outputs so that all values are in range (0, 1) and sum to 1.
It is often used as the activation for the last layer of a classification network because the result could be
interpreted as a probability distribution.
\:param inp: Input tensor.
\:return: Real-valued tensor of the applied activation function
Use the softmax_real_with_mult activation function then use the softmax activation.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.softmax_real_by_parameter
documentation: Use the softmax_real_with_avg activation function then use the softmax activation.

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.softmax_real_with_polar
documentation:
The softmax activation function transforms the outputs so that all values are in range (0, 1) and sum to 1.
It is often used as the activation for the last layer of a classification network because the result could be
interpreted as a probability distribution.
\:param inp: Input tensor.
\:return: Real-valued tensor of the applied activation function
Use the softmax activation function according to parameters

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.georgiou_cdbp
documentation:
Activation function proposed by G. M. Georgioy and C. Koutsougeras in
    https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/142037
Use the softmax_real_with_avg activation function for polar

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.complex_signum
documentation:
Complex signum activation function is very similar to mvn_activation.
For a detailed explanation refer to:
    https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/548176
Complex Backpropagation Activation Function

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.mvn_activation
documentation:
Function inspired by Naum Aizenberg.
    A multi-valued neuron (MVN) is a neural element with n inputs and one output lying on the unit circle,
    and with complex-valued weights.
Works:
    https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007%2FBF01068667
    http://pefmath2.etf.rs/files/93/399.pdf

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.apply_pol
documentation: Polar form

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.pol_tanh
documentation: polar form to complex number form

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.pol_sigmoid
documentation: Use the tanh activation function

functions.name: cv_net_library.activation.ComplexActivation.pol_selu
documentation: Use the sigmoid activation function
